Recreate the sizzling, aromatic, and intensely flavorful experience of traditional Karahi Gosht (or Kadhai Gosht) with Shan Karahi Gosht Masala 100g. This signature spice blend from Pakistan's renowned Shan brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is expertly crafted with a unique combination of whole and ground spices, specifically designed to help you achieve the authentic taste of this beloved stir-fried meat curry.

What is Karahi Gosht?

Karahi Gosht is a popular dish from the Indian subcontinent, particularly famous in Pakistani cuisine. "Gosht" refers to meat (typically mutton, lamb, or chicken) and "Karahi" is a type of deep, circular cooking pot similar to a wok. The dish is characterized by its rich, thick gravy, with prominent flavors of fresh tomatoes, ginger, garlic, green chillies, and a robust blend of coarsely ground spices. It's traditionally cooked and served in the same karahi.

Authentic Flavour, Simplified

  • The Taste of Lahore: Shan has perfected this masala to deliver the genuine, spicy, tangy, and deeply savory notes that make Karahi Gosht a restaurant and home-cooking favorite.
  • A Perfect Blend of Spices: Contains a harmonious mix of spices like coriander, red chilli, paprika, cumin, ginger, garlic, and often larger pieces of spices like dried red chillies or crushed coriander seeds for texture and aroma. This takes the guesswork out of your seasoning.
  • Ideal for High-Heat Cooking: The masala is designed to release its flavors beautifully when cooked with meat, tomatoes, and other ingredients in a karahi or similar pan over relatively high heat.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: Simplifies the process of making this flavorful dish by providing all the essential spices in one perfectly proportioned pack.
  • Trusted Shan Quality: A household name, Shan guarantees high-quality ingredients and authentic taste in every spice blend.

How to Use Shan Karahi Gosht Masala

(Shan's packaging includes detailed, easy-to-follow recipe instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Heat oil or ghee in a karahi or heavy-bottomed pan. Sauté chopped onions (if using, some recipes omit them). Add ginger-garlic paste and cook.
  2. Add your choice of meat (mutton, lamb, or chicken pieces) and brown it on high heat.
  3. Stir in chopped fresh tomatoes (a key ingredient) and the Shan Karahi Gosht Masala. Cook, stirring frequently, until the tomatoes soften and the oil begins to separate from the masala.
  4. Add a little water or yogurt if needed, cover, and cook until the meat is tender and the gravy has thickened to coat the meat pieces.
  5. Garnish generously with fresh julienned ginger, green chillies, and chopped coriander leaves.
  6. Serve piping hot directly from the karahi with hot naan, roti, or chapati.

Your Stockholm Destination for Authentic Pakistani Spices

At Ideal Indiska, your dedicated Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm, we are delighted to offer a wide range of authentic masalas from trusted brands like Shan. With our convenient local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and all of Europe, creating your favorite restaurant-style dishes at home has never been easier.

Product Details:

  • Brand: Shan Foods
  • Product: Karahi Gosht Masala (Kadhai Gosht Masala)
  • Weight: 100g
  • Form: Mix of Ground & some Whole/Broken Spices
  • Flavor Profile: Spicy, Aromatic, Savory, Tangy, with notes of Ginger, Garlic & Tomato
  • Key Ingredients: (Typically includes Red Chilli, Salt, Coriander, Paprika, Cinnamon, Dehydrated Onion, Ginger, Garlic, Turmeric, Cumin, Black Pepper, Green Cardamom, Bay Leaf, Clove, Brown Cardamom, Carom, Citric Acid, Maltodextrin, Hydrolyzed Soy Protein, Cane Sugar, Canola Oil, Silicon Dioxide (Anticaking Agent) - refer to packaging for the exact list by Shan).
  • Allergen Information: Contains Soy. May contain traces of Sulphite, Sesame, Mustard, Gluten, and Tree Nuts. (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate allergen information from Shan).
  • Origin: Pakistan
  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place in an airtight container or the sachet tightly sealed to retain its potent aroma and flavor.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Description:
The Everyday Hero of the South Indian Kitchen

Experience the delightful taste and light, fluffy texture of a true culinary staple with India Gate Sona Masoori Rice, available in a generous 5kg value pack at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. From the house of India Gate, a brand globally renowned for its commitment to quality rice, this premium Sona Masoori is the authentic choice for countless South Indian households and the secret to many beloved dishes. It is celebrated for its lightweight feel, pleasant aroma, and incredible versatility, making it the perfect rice for your everyday meals.

What is Sona Masoori Rice? Your Guide to This Versatile Grain

Sona Masoori (also spelled Sona Masuri) is a popular medium-grain rice, primarily cultivated in the fertile regions of Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka in India. It has earned its place as a kitchen essential for several key reasons:

  • Lightweight & Easy to Digest: When cooked, the grains become characteristically soft, fluffy, and separate. It's known for being lighter on the stomach compared to some other rice varieties, making it a preferred choice for daily consumption.
  • Pleasant, Subtle Aroma: Sona Masoori has a delicate and distinct aroma that is less potent than Basmati but wonderfully fragrant in its own right, enhancing the overall dining experience.
  • Lower Starch Content: It is naturally low in starch, which contributes to its non-sticky texture after cooking. This quality makes it a favourite for those who prefer fluffy rice that doesn't clump together.
  • The India Gate Standard of Quality: When you choose India Gate, you are choosing a brand that is a world leader in rice. Their Sona Masoori is hygienically packed, consistently aged (if applicable), and sorted to ensure you get clean, high-quality grains in every bag.

How is Sona Masoori Different from Basmati Rice?

This is a common question! While both are staples, they serve different purposes:

  • Grain Size: Sona Masoori is a medium-grain rice, while Basmati is a long-grain rice.
  • Texture: Sona Masoori is softer and lighter. Basmati is known for its extreme length and "fluffiness."
  • Best Use: Think of Sona Masoori as your versatile everyday hero for dals, sambar, and variety rice. Think of Basmati as your festive king for biryanis and pulaos where grain length is key (though Sona Masoori also makes excellent pulaos!).

The Perfect Rice for a World of Dishes

The versatility of India Gate Sona Masoori makes it a must-have in any well-stocked pantry. It's the ideal choice for:

  • Everyday Steamed Rice: The perfect, comforting accompaniment to all your favourite dals, curries, and sabzis. Its soft texture is wonderful for soaking up gravies.
  • South Indian Classics: The authentic choice for making Sambar Rice, Rasam Rice, and the cooling summer favourite, Curd Rice (Thayir Sadam).
  • Flavorful Variety Rice: Its ability to absorb flavors makes it perfect for Lemon Rice, Tamarind Rice (Pulihora), Coconut Rice, and Tomato Rice.
  • Pulao & Biryani: While different from Basmati, it makes a delicious and authentic South Indian-style biryani or pulao where a softer grain is often preferred.
  • Pongal & Khichdi: Its texture is ideal for creating creamy, comforting one-pot dishes like Pongal or Khichdi.

We are proud to offer key products like India Gate Sona Masoori Rice. How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Tapal is Pakistan's most-loved tea brand, crafting bold, full-bodied blends for the perfect desi cup since 1947. Each batch is blended to deliver a consistent, full-bodied cup that pairs perfectly with warm milk and aromatic spices — the hallmark of a great masala chai.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

About Wagh Bakri

Wagh Bakri has been blending premium Indian teas since 1892, winning generations of chai lovers across Gujarat and beyond. Each batch is blended to deliver a consistent, full-bodied cup that pairs perfectly with warm milk and aromatic spices — the hallmark of a great masala chai.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Name: Patak's Tandoori Paste 312g - Authentic Spice Paste for Grilling & BBQ
Short description:
Tandoori Paste

\nRecreate authentic, restaurant-style Tandoori flavors at home with Patak's Tandoori Paste! This 312g jar contains a rich, aromatic, and tangy blend of traditional Indian spices, ginger, garlic, and tamarind. Perfect for marinating chicken, paneer, fish, or vegetables for a delicious smoky taste when grilled, baked, or barbecued.
Description:
The Authentic Taste of the Tandoor, Simplified: Patak's Tandoori Paste.

Bring the celebrated, smoky, and aromatic flavors of traditional Indian tandoori cooking into your home kitchen with Patak's Tandoori Marinade Paste 312g. This expertly crafted paste from the globally renowned Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, offers a convenient and authentic blend of spices, ginger, garlic, tamarind, and natural colorants. It's designed to create the perfect marinade for chicken, meat, fish, paneer, or vegetables, infusing them with that irresistible tandoori taste before grilling, baking, or barbecuing.

 Understanding Tandoori  Paste.
Tandoori cooking traditionally involves marinating food in a yogurt and spice mixture before cooking at high heat in a cylindrical clay oven (tandoor). Patak's Tandoori Paste provides the essential spice base for this marinade.

  • Authentic Tandoori Flavor Profile: Carefully blended to deliver the characteristic tangy, spicy (usually mild to medium), smoky, and deeply aromatic notes of classic tandoori dishes.
  • Rich Blend of Spices: Typically includes ginger, garlic, tamarind for tang, coriander, cumin, paprika (for color), garam masala spices, and other traditional Indian condiments.
  • Perfect for Marinades: Designed to be mixed, often with yogurt, to create a thick marinade that clings to food, tenderizes it, and infuses it with flavor.
  • Vibrant Natural Colour: Often imparts an appealing reddish-orange hue to the marinated food, reminiscent of tandoor-cooked dishes.
  • Versatile for Grilling, Baking, or BBQ: While traditionally for tandoor, this paste is excellent for modern cooking methods, achieving delicious results on your home grill, in the oven, or on the barbecue.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces the effort of sourcing and grinding numerous individual spices for your tandoori marinade.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a long-standing reputation for producing high-quality, authentic Indian food products.

 How to Use Patak's Tandoori Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide detailed usage instructions and recipe ideas.

  1. For approximately 4 servings, mix 2-3 tablespoons of Patak's Tandoori Marinade Paste with 2-3 tablespoons of plain yogurt (optional, but traditional for creaminess and tenderizing). You can also add a squeeze of lemon juice.
  2. Score your chosen protein (e.g., chicken pieces, paneer cubes, fish fillets) or prepare your vegetables (e.g., cauliflower florets, bell pepper chunks, onions).
  3. Coat the protein/vegetables thoroughly with the tandoori marinade.
  4. Cover and refrigerate for at least 1-2 hours, or preferably for several hours (or overnight for meat) to allow the flavors to penetrate deeply.
  5. Cook as desired:
  • Grill/BBQ: Thread onto skewers and grill over medium-hot coals or on a barbecue until cooked through and nicely charred.
  • Oven: Bake in a preheated oven (around 200°C/400°F) until cooked through.
  • Pan-Fry: Can also be pan-fried.
  • Serve hot with naan, rice, salad, and mint chutney.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic Patak's Marinades.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a wide range of Patak's authentic cooking pastes, including this flavorful Tandoori Marinade Paste. Make your grilling and BBQ sessions in Stockholm extra special with the taste of India!

Product Details:

  • Brand: Patak's
  • Product: Tandoori Marinade Paste
  • Weight/Volume: 312g
  • Form: Thick Spice Paste
  • Flavor Profile: Tangy, Aromatic, Smoky (implied tandoori), Mildly to Medium Spicy
  • Key Ingredients: Water, Ginger Purée, Ground Spices (Coriander, Cumin, Paprika, Spices), Tamarind, Salt, Acids (Acetic Acid, Lactic Acid), Garlic Powder, Colours (E160c, E122, E129 - it is CRUCIAL to list specific colorants as per packaging). (This is a typical Patak's Tandoori Paste ingredient list - verify and list accurately from the specific product packaging you stock.)
  • Allergen Information: May contain Mustard, Peanuts, and Nuts. (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate and complete allergen information from Patak's).
  • Dietary Information: Vegetarian (Often Vegan, but verify if any dairy derivatives are present, though usually not in the paste itself).
  • Origin: (Typically UK for Patak's products)
  • Storage: Store in a cool, dry place. Once opened, refrigerate and use within a specified period (e.g., 6 months, or by best before date – check jar).

Name: Patak's Madras Curry Paste 283g - Hot & Tangy South Indian Spice Blend
Short description:
Patak's Madras Curry Paste

\nIgnite your taste buds with the authentic, fiery heat of Patak's Madras Curry Paste! This 283g jar contains a rich and aromatic blend of chillies, coriander, cumin, tamarind, and other classic South Indian spices. Perfect for creating robust and tangy Madras curries with chicken, lamb, beef, or vegetables.
Description:
The Fiery Flavours of South India: Patak's Madras Curry Paste.

Experience the bold, vibrant, and satisfyingly spicy taste of authentic South Indian cuisine with Patak's Madras Curry Paste 283g. This expertly crafted paste from the globally renowned Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, captures the essence of a traditional Madras curry – a rich, hot, and tangy blend of aromatic spices, chillies, and tamarind. It's the perfect base for creating deeply flavorful curries that pack a delightful punch.

 Understanding Madras Curry.
Originating from the city of Madras (now Chennai) in South India, Madras curry is characterized by its generous use of chillies, a distinct tanginess (often from tamarind), and a complex blend of spices that typically includes coriander, cumin, turmeric, fenugreek, and mustard seeds.

  • Authentic Hot & Tangy Flavor: Patak's Madras Paste is carefully balanced to deliver a genuine Madras curry taste that is robustly spicy, tangy, and deeply aromatic.
  • Rich Blend of Spices & Chillies: Features a classic mix of ground spices, with a prominent chilli content for significant heat, complemented by the sourness of tamarind and the earthiness of other traditional spices.
  • Versatile for Various Proteins & Vegetables: While excellent for Chicken Madras or Lamb Madras, this paste also creates wonderfully spicy and flavorful curries with beef, fish, prawns, or a medley of vegetables.
  • Builds a Deeply Flavored Base: Provides a concentrated flavor foundation, allowing you to create restaurant-quality Madras curry at home with ease.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces preparation time, eliminating the need to source, toast, and grind numerous individual spices.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a rich heritage in producing authentic Indian pastes, pickles, and sauces, trusted by cooks around the world for consistent quality and taste.

 How to Use Patak's Madras Curry Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide recipe suggestions. A general guide is below.

  1. Heat a little oil in a pan. Sauté chopped onions until soft (optional).
  2. Add your choice of diced chicken, meat, seafood, or vegetables and stir-fry for a few minutes.
  3. Stir in Patak's Madras Curry Paste (typically 2-3 tablespoons per serving, adjust to your heat preference and as per jar instructions) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant, ensuring it doesn't burn.
  4. Add other ingredients like chopped tomatoes, coconut milk (to temper heat if desired), or stock to create your desired sauce consistency. Bring to a simmer.
  5. Cover and cook gently until your main ingredients are tender and cooked through, and the sauce has thickened.
  6. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
  7. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, roti, or idiyappam (string hoppers).

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Authentic & Spicy Patak's Pastes.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a range of Patak's authentic curry pastes, including this fiery and flavorful Madras Curry Paste. Spice up your meals and explore the bold tastes of South India in your Stockholm kitchen!

Name: Patak's Garam Masala Paste 283g - Aromatic & Warming Indian Spice Paste
Short description:
Patak's Garam Masala Paste.

\n

Add authentic Indian warmth and complex aroma to your dishes with Patak's Garam Masala Paste! This 283g jar contains a perfectly balanced blend of traditional Garam Masala spices in a convenient paste form. Ideal for easily incorporating into curries, dals, lentil soups, marinades, or as a flavorful finishing touch
Description:
The Heart of Indian Flavour, in a Convenient Paste: Patak's Garam Masala Paste. 

Experience the quintessential aromatic warmth of traditional Indian Garam Masala in an easy-to-use paste with Patak's Garam Masala Paste 283g. This innovative product from the globally trusted Patak's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, blends a harmonious selection of classic Garam Masala spices into a convenient paste, ready to infuse your curries, dals, marinades, and other dishes with a rich, complex, and deeply satisfying flavor.

 Understanding Garam Masala Paste.
Garam Masala, meaning "hot/warming spice blend," is a cornerstone of Indian cuisine, typically used as a finishing powder. Patak's Garam Masala Paste offers the same aromatic profile in a different format:

  • Authentic Garam Masala Flavours: Carefully blended to deliver the characteristic warm, sweet, and savory notes of spices like cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, coriander, and black pepper.
  • Convenient Paste Form: Unlike dry powder, this paste can be easily stirred into dishes during various stages of cooking, blending seamlessly with other ingredients. It may also contain oil or other wet ingredients that help carry the spice flavors.
  • Aromatic & Warming: Imparts a deep, comforting aroma and a gentle warming sensation (not necessarily chilli heat) to your food, characteristic of good Garam Masala.
  • Enhances a Wide Range of Dishes: Perfect for adding a final flourish of authentic Indian flavor to vegetarian and non-vegetarian curries, lentil preparations, rice dishes, and even some snacks.
  • Time-Saving Solution: Saves you from grinding individual whole spices, while offering a more integrated flavor than simply sprinkling powder at the end (depending on how it's used).
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's commitment to authentic flavors ensures this paste delivers a genuine taste experience.

How to Use Patak's Garam Masala Paste.

Patak's jars typically provide usage suggestions. A general guide is below.

  • During Cooking: Stir a tablespoon or two (to taste) into your curry, dal, or vegetable dish towards the middle or end of the cooking process to allow the flavors to meld.
  • In Marinades: Add to yogurt or oil-based marinades for chicken, lamb, paneer, or vegetables to infuse them with a warm, aromatic spice.
  • As a Finishing Touch: While a paste, a small amount can still be stirred in right before serving to boost the aroma.
  • Sauté with Onions & Tomatoes: Can be added along with ginger and garlic paste when sautéing the base for your curries.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Innovative Patak's Products.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a range of Patak's authentic Indian cooking solutions, including this versatile Garam Masala Paste. Simplify your cooking and enhance the flavor of your Indian meals in Stockholm with this convenient and aromatic paste.

Name: Heera Kalonji Seeds 300g
Short description:
Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds/Black Onion Seeds) 300g - Pungent & Aromatic Spice

\n

Add a unique, pungent, and slightly peppery flavor to your dishes with Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds). This 300g pack contains small, black, tear-drop shaped seeds, essential for sprinkling on naan bread, flavouring authentic Indian pickles (achaar), and for use in tempering (tadka) for various curries and vegetable dishes.
Description:
The Distinctive Pungent Seed of Flavour

Discover the uniquely aromatic and pungent flavour of Heera Kalonji (Nigella Seeds) in a generous 300g pack. This essential spice, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of small, black, tear-drop shaped seeds that are often mistakenly called "black onion seeds" or "black caraway," but are in fact from the Nigella sativa plant. Kalonji is a staple in Indian, Pakistani, Middle Eastern, and Polish cuisines, prized for its distinctive taste that is a complex blend of oregano, black pepper, and a slight bitterness.

Understanding Kalonji (Nigella Seeds)

Kalonji has a rich history both in the kitchen and in traditional wellness practices. Its flavour is best released when it is dry-roasted or bloomed in hot oil.

  • Unique Pungent Flavor: Offers a complex taste that is sharp, slightly bitter, peppery, and highly aromatic. It should not be confused with black sesame seeds or black cumin.
  • Essential for Naan Bread: One of its most iconic uses is being sprinkled on top of naan bread before baking, where it imparts a beautiful look and a characteristic pungent aroma.
  • Key Pickling Spice: A vital component in many traditional Indian pickles (achaar), especially those with mango, lime, and chillies. It's a key ingredient in the famous Bengali five-spice blend, Panch Phoron.
  • Used in Tempering (Tadka): In some regional cuisines, kalonji is added to hot oil or ghee along with other spices to create a flavorful base for lentil dishes (dals) and vegetable curries (sabzis).
  • Traditional Wellness Beliefs: In Islamic and Ayurvedic traditions, Kalonji (often called "black seed") is highly revered and believed to possess numerous health benefits.
  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera provides high-quality, clean kalonji seeds, ensuring authentic taste and aroma for your culinary needs.
  • Value 300g Pack: This larger pack is perfect for those who use Kalonji regularly in their cooking or for making pickles.

Culinary Uses for Heera Kalonji

  • Breads: Sprinkle generously over naan, parathas, or other flatbreads before cooking.
  • Pickles (Achaar): A fundamental spice in the masala for a wide variety of Indian pickles.
  • Vegetable Dishes (Sabzis): Adds a unique flavor to stir-fried or slow-cooked vegetable dishes, especially with potatoes, pumpkin, or eggplant.
  • Lentil Dishes (Dals): Used in the tempering (tadka) for some regional dal preparations.
  • Spice Blends: A key ingredient in Bengali Panch Phoron.
  • Savory Pastries & Snacks: Can be sprinkled on or mixed into the dough for savory snacks like mathri or kachori.

Tips for Using Kalonji

  • To bring out its full flavour, lightly dry-roast the seeds in a pan for a minute or two until they become fragrant before using.
  • Use in moderation, as its flavor is quite strong and can become overpowering if used in excess.

Name: TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g - Aromatic & Delicate Spice
Short description:
TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g

\n

Add a warm, delicate, and subtly sweet-spicy aroma to your gourmet dishes with TRS Javentri (Whole Mace). This 50g pack contains the lacy, red-orange outer covering of the nutmeg seed, an essential spice in rich biryanis, kormas, kebabs, and authentic Mughlai cooking.
Description:
The Delicate Sibling of Nutmeg: TRS Javentri (Whole Mace)

Discover the elegant and aromatic secret of gourmet Indian and Pakistani cooking with TRS Javentri (Whole Mace Blades) 50g. This unique and prized spice, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is the lacy, net-like covering (known as an aril) that surrounds the nutmeg seed. While related to nutmeg, Mace has its own distinct character – a warmer, more delicate, and slightly sweeter flavor, often described as a combination of cinnamon and pepper with a floral hint.

The Allure of Whole Javentri (Mace)

Mace is a cornerstone of rich, aromatic cuisines, particularly Mughlai, and is valued by discerning chefs for its sophisticated flavor profile.

  • Warm & Aromatic Flavor: Imparts a complex, warm, and fragrant aroma that is both sweet and savory. It is less pungent than nutmeg, offering a more refined and delicate note.
  • Essential for Royal Cuisine (Mughlai): A key ingredient in the spice blends for authentic Shahi Korma, Biryani, Pulao, and rich meat dishes. It adds a signature royal fragrance.
  • Perfect for Kebabs: Ground mace is often used in the marinades for succulent kebabs, like Galouti or Seekh Kebabs, for its unique flavor and aroma.
  • Component of Garam Masala: A vital part of many traditional and homemade Garam Masala blends, contributing to their complexity.
  • Superior Freshness in Whole Form: Keeping mace in its whole blade form preserves its volatile oils and delicate aroma far better than pre-ground powder. Grind just what you need for the freshest, most potent flavor.

How to Use TRS Javentri (Mace)

  • For Grinding: Lightly toast a blade of Javentri in a dry pan for a few seconds to make it more brittle and enhance its aroma. Let it cool, then grind in a spice grinder or with a mortar and pestle. A small amount is usually sufficient.
  • For Infusion: You can add a whole blade or a large piece to the oil/ghee during the initial tempering (tadka) or directly into the simmering liquid of a korma or biryani preparation to infuse the dish with its aroma. It is typically removed before serving.

Name: Haldiram's Chakoli (Crispy Rice Flour Spirals) 200g
Short description:
Chakoli (Crispy Rice Flour Spirals) 200g - Savory Indian Snack

Enjoy the irresistible, crunchy goodness of Haldiram's Chakoli! This 200g pack offers a classic Indian savory snack (namkeen), featuring spiral-shaped, deep-fried crisps made from rice flour and a blend of mild spices. Perfectly crunchy and delicious, it's the ideal snack for tea time, parties, or any time cravings strike.
Description:
The Irresistible Crunchy Spiral: Haldiram's Chakoli

Experience the delightful and satisfying crunch of a true Indian classic with Haldiram's Chakoli in a convenient 200g pack. This beloved savory snack ("namkeen") from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a deep-fried, spiral-shaped crisp made primarily from rice flour and a subtle blend of traditional spices. Its unique shape, incredible crunch, and savory flavor make it an all-time favorite snack.

What is Chakoli (Chakli/Murukku)?

Chakoli, known as Chakli in Maharashtra and Murukku in South India, is a traditional snack cherished across the country, especially during festivals like Diwali. It's made from a dough of rice flour, sometimes mixed with other flours like gram flour (besan) or urad dal flour, which is then extruded into its characteristic spiral or coil shape and deep-fried to golden perfection.

  • Incredibly Crunchy & Crispy: The hallmark of a good Chakole is its satisfyingly loud crunch, and Haldiram's delivers on this perfectly.
  • Mildly Spiced & Savory: Seasoned with a gentle blend of spices that often includes cumin, sesame seeds, and a hint of chilli, making it flavorful but not overly spicy. It's a taste that is loved by all ages.
  • Authentic Traditional Snack: Captures the authentic taste and texture of homemade Chakolee, saving you the effort of making this intricate snack from scratch.
  • The Perfect Tea-Time Companion (Chai Time): Its savory and crunchy nature makes it an ideal partner for a hot cup of Indian tea or coffee.
  • Great for Parties & Gatherings: A crowd-pleasing snack that is perfect to serve to guests or to include in a festive snack platter.
  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: From a brand renowned for its commitment to quality, hygiene, and authentic Indian flavors.

How to Enjoy Your Haldiram's Chakoli

Haldiram's Chakoli is a ready-to-eat snack that is perfect for any occasion:

  • Enjoy it straight from the pack for a satisfying, crunchy treat.
  • Serve with tea, coffee, or a cold beverage as an evening snack.
  • Add it to your "namkeen" platter when hosting guests.
  • Pack it as a convenient and tasty on-the-go snack.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Haldiram's Snacks

With our convenient local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and Europe, you can easily stock up on the authentic tastes you love.

Description:
The Classic Taste of Celebration: Haldiram's Besan Ladoo

Experience the authentic and heartwarming sweetness of a true Indian classic with Haldiram's Besan Ladoo 400g, a signature mithai from India's renowned Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. These golden, spherical delights are crafted from slow-roasted gram flour (besan), pure ghee (clarified butter), and sugar, creating a uniquely nutty, rich, and melt-in-your-mouth texture that has been cherished for generations.

 

 What Makes Haldiram's Besan Ladoo So Special?

Besan Ladoo is more than just a sweet; it's a symbol of joy, celebration, and tradition in Indian culture. Haldiram's stays true to the authentic recipe, ensuring every ladoo is a bite of bliss.

  • Authentic Recipe & Taste: Haldiram's uses a traditional recipe, perfectly roasting the besan to develop its characteristic nutty aroma and flavor, then skillfully blending it with high-quality ghee and the right amount of sweetness.

  • Melt-in-Your-Mouth Texture: The slow roasting and careful preparation result in a ladoo that is delightfully soft and crumbly, dissolving beautifully on the palate.

  • Rich & Nutty Flavor: The combination of roasted gram flour and pure ghee imparts a distinctive, rich, and satisfyingly nutty taste.

  • Subtly Aromatic: Often lightly flavored with cardamom (elaichi) and sometimes garnished with nuts like pistachios or almonds, adding a delicate fragrance.

  • Perfect for All Occasions: Whether it's Diwali, Raksha Bandhan, a wedding, a birth announcement, or simply a desire for a delicious traditional sweet, Besan Ladoo is always a welcome treat.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Enjoy the consistent quality, hygiene, and authentic taste that Haldiram's is famous for.

 Suggestion: Serving Your Besan Ladoo

Haldiram's Besan Ladoos are ready to eat and enjoy:

  • Serve them as a dessert after a meal.

  • Offer them to guests during festive gatherings or celebrations.

  • Enjoy one with a cup of tea or coffee for a delightful afternoon treat.

  • They make a wonderful gift for friends and family.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Name: Haldiram's Frozen Veg Shami Kebab (Vegetarian Spiced Patties) 300g - Authentic Flavour
Short description:
Haldiram's Frozen Veg Shami Kebab

\n

Enjoy the rich, aromatic taste of Shami Kebabs in a delicious vegetarian version with Haldiram's Frozen Veg Shami Kebabs! This 300g pack contains flavorful, mildly spiced patties made from lentils and/or vegetables. Ready to shallow fry or bake for a perfect appetizer, snack, or side dish.
Description:
The Authentic Kebab Experience, Vegetarian Style: Haldiram's Frozen Veg Shami Kebab - 300g)

Savor the delightful flavors of a classic Mughlai appetizer, reimagined for vegetarians, with Haldiram's Frozen Veg Shami Kebab (Vegetarian Spiced Patties) 300g. This convenient ready-to-cook offering from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you tender, flavorful patties inspired by traditional Shami Kebabs, made with a wholesome blend of lentils, vegetables, and aromatic spices.

 Understanding Veg Shami Kebab.

Traditional Shami Kebabs are made with minced meat (usually lamb or beef) and chana dal (split chickpeas), along with a host of spices. Haldiram's Veg Shami Kebab offers a delicious plant-based alternative that captures the essence of this beloved dish:

  • Authentic Kebab Flavor: Crafted to deliver a taste profile reminiscent of classic Shami Kebabs, with a rich, savory, and mildly spiced character.

  • Wholesome Vegetarian Ingredients: Typically made with a base of lentils (like chana dal), vegetables (such as potatoes, carrots, peas), and sometimes soya or paneer, bound together with spices.

  • Soft & Tender Texture: Designed to have a melt-in-your-mouth texture on the inside with a slightly crisp exterior when cooked.

  • Ready to Cook Convenience: These frozen kebabs save you the intricate process of grinding lentils, mixing spices, and shaping patties from scratch.

  • Versatile Cooking Methods: Can be shallow fried, pan-fried, baked, or air fried to achieve your desired finish.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: From a brand renowned for its authentic Indian snacks and sweets, ensuring a quality product.

  • Perfect Appetizer or Snack: Ideal as a starter for Indian meals, a party appetizer, a satisfying tea-time snack, or even as a filling for wraps and sandwiches.

 Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Shallow Fry (Recommended): Heat a small amount of oil or ghee in a non-stick pan. Place the frozen Veg Shami Kebabs and cook on medium-low heat for about 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown, crispy, and heated through.

  • Pan Fry: Similar to shallow frying, with minimal oil.

  • Bake/Air Fry (Healthier Option): Preheat your oven or air fryer. Lightly brush or spray the kebabs with oil. Bake at around 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes or air fry at 180-200°C for 10-15 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden brown and heated through.

Serve hot with green mint-coriander chutney, tamarind chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Delicious Vegetarian Frozen Snacks.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a variety of Haldiram's convenient frozen snacks, including these flavorful Veg Shami Kebabs. Enjoy a taste of authentic Indian appetizers with ease in your Stockholm home.

Product Details:

  • Brand: Haldiram's

  • Product: Veg Shami Kebab (Vegetarian Spiced Patties) - Frozen

  • Weight: 300g

  • Form: Frozen, Ready-to-Cook Vegetarian Patties

  • Flavor Profile: Savory, Mildly Spiced, Aromatic, with notes of Lentils/Vegetables & Indian Spices

  • Key Ingredients: Vegetables (e.g., Potatoes, Carrots, Peas), Lentils (e.g., Split Chickpeas/Chana Dal), Breadcrumbs (or other binders), Spices & Condiments (e.g., Ginger, Garlic, Green Chilli, Coriander, Cumin, Garam Masala), Salt, Edible Vegetable Oil. (Refer to Haldiram's packaging for the exact and complete ingredient list).

  • Allergen Information: Contains Wheat (Gluten from breadcrumbs, if used). May contain traces of Milk, Soy, Nuts, Sesame. (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate allergen information from Haldiram's).

  • Dietary Information: Vegetarian

  • Origin: India

  • Storage: Keep frozen at -18°C or below. Do not refreeze once thawed. Cook from frozen as per instructions.

Name: Haldiram's Frozen Punjabi Samosas (Spiced Potato & Pea Pastries) approx. 8 pcs
Short description:
Haldiram's Frozen Punjabi Samosas | 8Pc

\n

Savor the authentic taste of classic Punjabi Samosas with Haldiram's Frozen pack! This pack (approx. 8 pcs ) contains generously sized, triangular crispy pastries filled with a traditional savory blend of spiced potatoes and green peas. Perfect as a hearty snack, appetizer, or tea-time treat. Just fry or bake from frozen!

\n
Description:
The Irresistible & Authentic: Haldiram's Frozen Punjabi Samosas - 8Pc)

Indulge in one of India's most beloved and iconic savory snacks with Haldiram's Frozen Punjabi Samosas (approx. 8 pieces / 650g). This convenient offering from the renowned Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you perfectly shaped, ready-to-cook triangular pastries. Each samosa is generously filled with a classic, flavorful, and mildly spiced mixture of potatoes and green peas, capturing the authentic taste of traditional Punjabi Samosas.

 The True Punjabi Samosa Experience.

Punjabi Samosas are cherished for their crispy, flaky pastry and their hearty, savory filling. Haldiram's ensures this traditional essence is preserved:

  • Classic Savory Filling: Features a delicious and traditional filling of mashed potatoes and tender green peas, perfectly seasoned with a blend of aromatic Indian spices like cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, and a hint of tanginess.

  • Crispy Golden Pastry: Designed to fry or bake to a delightful golden brown with a satisfyingly crispy and substantial texture.

  • Generously Sized: These are the classic, hearty Punjabi Samosas, perfect for a satisfying snack or appetizer.

  • Ultimate Convenience - Ready to Cook: These frozen samosas save you the intricate and time-consuming process of making samosa pastry and filling from scratch.

  • Cooks from Frozen: Easily cooked by deep frying, baking, or air frying directly from the freezer for a quick and impressive treat.

  • Ideal Pack Size: With approximately 8 pieces (650g), this pack is great for family snacks or small gatherings.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: Haldiram's is a globally recognized name for quality, hygiene, and authentic Indian snacks and sweets.

 Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Deep Fry (Recommended for best traditional results): Heat oil in a deep pan or kadai to medium-hot. Gently slide the frozen samosas into the hot oil (do not overcrowd). Fry on medium-low heat for about 8-12 minutes (as they are larger than cocktail samosas), turning occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy on all sides, and the filling is heated through. Drain on absorbent paper.

  • Bake (Healthier Option): Preheat oven to around 200°C (400°F). Lightly brush or spray the frozen samosas with oil. Place them on a baking tray and bake for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and heated through, flipping them halfway.

  • Air Fry: Preheat your air fryer. Lightly brush or spray the frozen samosas with oil. Cook according to your air fryer's instructions (typically around 180-200°C for 15-20 minutes), flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.

Serve hot with mint chutney, tamarind chutney, or your favorite Indian dipping sauces. They are also delicious with a hot cup of chai!

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Classic Indian Frozen Snacks.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a variety of Haldiram's convenient frozen snacks, including these popular Punjabi Samosas. Bring the authentic taste of India's favorite snack to your Stockholm home with ease!

  • Key Ingredients: Pastry (Wheat Flour, Water, Edible Vegetable Oil, Salt), Filling (Potatoes, Green Peas, Edible Vegetable Oil, Spices & Condiments (Coriander, Cumin, Ginger, Green Chilli, Mango Powder, Garam Masala, etc.), Salt). Name: Haldiram's Frozen Aloo Tikki (Spiced Potato Patties) 420g - Authentic Street Food Snack
Short description:
Haldiram's Frozen Aloo Tikki

\n

Enjoy the iconic taste of authentic Aloo Tikki with Haldiram's! This 420g frozen pack contains delicious, mildly spiced potato patties, ready to be shallow fried, deep fried, or air fried to a golden crisp. Perfect for making Aloo Tikki Chaat, as a burger patty, or a delightful savory snack.
Description:
The Heart of Indian Street Food: Haldiram's Frozen Aloo Tikki - 420g)

Experience the irresistible flavors and comforting texture of authentic North Indian Aloo Tikki with Haldiram's Frozen Aloo Tikki (Spiced Potato Patties) 420g. This convenient ready-to-cook offering from India's iconic Haldiram's brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, brings you perfectly seasoned mashed potato patties, ready to be transformed into a delicious snack, a key component of a vibrant chaat, or even a tasty vegetarian burger patty.

 The Timeless Appeal of Aloo Tikki.

Aloo Tikki, meaning "potato patty" or "potato cutlet," is a beloved street food and snack across India. It's known for its soft interior and often crispy exterior, with a delightful blend of spices.

  • Classic Comfort Food Flavor: Haldiram's captures the traditional, comforting taste of Aloo Tikki, with a balanced blend of spices that is flavorful yet generally mild.

  • Made with Potatoes & Spices: Features mashed potatoes combined with typical Indian spices like cumin, coriander, turmeric, ginger, green chillies, and sometimes breadcrumbs or gram flour for binding.

  • Versatile Snack & Chaat Base: The quintessential base for Aloo Tikki Chaat (topped with yogurt, chutneys, onions, sev), but also delicious on its own or with simple dips.

  • Ready to Cook Convenience: These frozen tikkis save you the significant time and effort of boiling, mashing, seasoning, and shaping potato patties from scratch.

  • Multiple Cooking Options: Can be shallow fried for a softer exterior, deep fried for ultimate crispness, or air fried/baked for a healthier option.

  • Trusted Haldiram's Quality: From a brand renowned for its authentic Indian snacks and sweets, ensuring a quality product.

  • Perfect for All Ages: Its generally mild spice level makes it a hit with both adults and children.

Easy Cooking Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  • Deep Fry (Traditional & Crispiest): Heat oil in a pan or kadai. Gently slide the frozen Aloo Tikkis into the hot oil. Fry on medium heat, turning occasionally, for about 5-7 minutes or until golden brown and crispy on both sides. Drain on absorbent paper.

  • Shallow Fry: Heat a few tablespoons of oil in a flat pan. Place the frozen tikkis and cook on medium heat, flipping after 3-4 minutes, until both sides are golden brown, crispy, and heated through.

  • Air Fry/Bake (Healthier Option): Preheat your air fryer or oven. Lightly brush or spray the tikkis with oil. Cook according to your appliance's instructions until golden brown and crispy. (Air frying approx. 180-200°C for 12-18 mins, flipping halfway. Baking approx. 200°C for 20-25 mins, flipping halfway).

Serve hot. For Aloo Tikki Chaat, top with whisked yogurt (dahi), tamarind chutney (imli chutney), mint-coriander chutney, chopped onions, tomatoes, sev, and a sprinkle of chaat masala. Also great with tomato ketchup or as a veggie burger patty.

 Your Stockholm Go-To for Iconic Indian Frozen Snacks.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a variety of Haldiram's convenient frozen snacks, including these delicious Aloo Tikkis. Enjoy a taste of authentic Indian street food and easy snacking in your Stockholm home.

Experience the taste that has been a cherished part of countless memories with the Maggi 2-Minute Masala Noodles Family Pack (6 x 70g). Perfect for students, busy professionals, and families, Maggi Masala Noodles are the ultimate go-to for a satisfying meal that's ready in a flash.

The Iconic Maggi Masala Taste

For decades, the magic of Maggi has been its simplicity and its unforgettable flavour. This pack brings you that classic experience:

  • Signature Masala Tastemaker: The heart and soul of the dish. Each pack comes with a sachet of the secret "Tastemaker" masala, a unique and aromatic blend of roasted Indian spices that creates a delicious, savory, and slightly spicy broth.
  • Ready in Just 2 Minutes: True to its name, Maggi noodles are incredibly quick and easy to prepare. Just boil, add the noodles and tastemaker, and your delicious meal is ready.
  • Versatile Culinary Canvas: While delicious on its own, Maggi is famous for being a base for creativity. Customize it by adding your favourite vegetables like peas, carrots, and onions, or make it more substantial with a fried egg, paneer, or tofu.

  • A Taste of Home & Nostalgia: For many in the Indian and Pakistani diaspora, the taste of Maggi Masala Noodles is a comforting reminder of home, childhood, and happy times.

Simple Cooking Instructions

  1. Break the noodle cake into four pieces.
  2. Add the noodles and the Tastemaker sachet to approximately 210ml (1.5 teacups) of boiling water.
  3. Cook for 2 minutes in an open pan, stirring occasionally. Do not drain the remaining water.
  4. Let it stand for 1 minute after cooking. Serve hot and enjoy!

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Name: Vandevi Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g
Short description:
Vandevi Compounded Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g - Authentic & Pungent Spice

\n

Add an authentic, savory, and pungent depth to your Indian dishes with Vandevi Hing (Asafoetida) Powder. This 50g container holds a strong, aromatic compounded asafoetida, an essential spice for tempering (tadka) in dals, enhancing vegetable curries, and aiding digestion. A must-have in every Indian spice box.
Description:
The Unmistakable Aroma of Authentic Indian Cooking

Unlock a fundamental flavor of authentic Indian cuisine with Vandevi Compounded Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g. This iconic spice from the trusted Vandevi brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a cornerstone of vegetarian cooking. It is prized for its strong, pungent, and savory aroma that blossoms when cooked, adding a deep, umami-rich flavor that wonderfully mimics the depth of onions and garlic. Just a tiny pinch is enough to transform a simple dish into a flavorful masterpiece.

Understanding Hing (Asafoetida): A Cook's Secret Weapon

Hing is a dried latex (gum oleoresin) exuded from the root of several species of Ferula, a perennial herb. While its raw aroma is famously potent, its true magic is revealed in the cooking process.

  • What is "Compounded" Hing? Pure asafoetida resin is incredibly strong. Vandevi's "compounded" hing is expertly blended with edible flour (like rice or wheat flour) and often turmeric (which can give it a yellowish tint). This traditional process makes the potent resin easy to use in powdered form and helps to evenly distribute its flavor.
  • The Ultimate Flavor Enhancer: It adds a deep, savory, umami-rich flavor that is especially crucial in vegetarian and lentil-based dishes where onions and garlic may not be used (such as in Jain or certain Sattvic cooking).
  • Essential for Tempering (Tadka): A key ingredient in the "tadka" or "chaunk." Adding a pinch of hing to hot oil or ghee along with other spices like cumin or mustard seeds creates an incredibly aromatic and flavorful foundation for dals, sambar, and curries.
  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating and gas, making it an almost inseparable partner for legumes and lentils to improve their digestibility.

Why Choose Vandevi Hing?

When you search for authentic Indian spices in Stockholm, a quality hing is non-negotiable. Vandevi is a well-recognized and trusted brand for high-quality, potent Hing. Its strength and purity mean that this small 50g container will last for a considerable time in a home kitchen, making it a very economical choice for adding immense flavor.

How to Use Vandevi Hing

Remember, Hing should always be cooked to release its true flavor. Never sprinkle it raw on finished dishes.

  1. Heat your ghee or oil in a pan for tempering (tadka).
  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just the amount you can pick up on the tip of a knife) of Vandevi Hing Powder.
  3. It will sizzle and become aromatic in just a few seconds. Immediately add your other whole spices (like cumin seeds) and proceed with your recipe by adding lentils, vegetables, etc.

It is a classic addition to almost all lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), beans (like chana masala), and in the masala for savory snacks and pickles.

Your Authentic Spice Source in Stockholm & Europe

At Ideal Indiska, we are committed to bringing you the essential ingredients that define authentic cooking. Vandevi Hing is one of those secret ingredients that makes all the difference. We are proud to offer this essential spice with delivery across Stockholm, Sweden, and all of Europe.

  • Allergen Information: May contain Wheat (Gluten). (Always check the specific product packaging for the most accurate allergen information, as the base flour can vary).

Name: Heera Semolina Coarse (Moti Suji/Ladoo Rava) 375g - For Upma, Ladoo & Halwa
Short description:
Heera Semolina Coarse

\nAchieve the perfect granular texture in your favorite Indian dishes with Heera Semolina Coarse (Moti Suji/Ladoo Rava). This 375g pack contains coarsely granulated durum wheat semolina, ideal for making fluffy Upma, grainy Sooji Halwa, delicious Rava Ladoos, and for use in batters to add extra crispness.
Description:
The Perfect Grain for Texture: Heera Semolina Coarse (Moti Suji)

Discover the secret to authentic texture in many classic Indian dishes with Heera Semolina Coarse (Moti Suji/Ladoo Rava) 375g. This high-quality, coarsely granulated semolina, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made from purified durum wheat middlings. Its larger grain size, compared to fine semolina, makes it the preferred choice for recipes where a distinct, grainy, and non-pasty texture is desired, such as in traditional Upma, Halwa, and Ladoos.

 The Coarse Semolina Advantage

While fine semolina is great for smooth preparations, coarse semolina (often called Moti Suji, meaning "pearl semolina") offers unique benefits:

  • Distinct Granular Texture: The larger granules remain more separate when cooked, providing a pleasing, non-sticky, and grainy mouthfeel, which is essential for authentic Upma and some styles of Halwa.
  • Ideal for Sweets like Ladoo: Often referred to as "Ladoo Rava," its texture is perfect for making Rava Ladoos, where the individual grains, bound with sugar and ghee, are a key part of the experience.
  • Adds Extra Crispness: When used in batters or as a coating for fried items, the coarse grains provide a superior, crunchy texture.
  • Absorbs Flavors While Retaining Texture: Soaks up the flavors of spices and liquids without breaking down completely and becoming mushy.
  • Versatile Kitchen Ingredient: A staple in many Indian households for a variety of breakfast dishes, sweets, and snacks.
  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring consistent granulation and quality for your culinary needs.

Culinary Uses for Heera Semolina Coarse

  • Upma: The quintessential choice for making fluffy, separate-grained savory breakfast porridge with vegetables and spices.
  • Rava Ladoo: The perfect base for making these popular sweet balls with ghee, sugar, and nuts.
  • Sooji Halwa / Rava Kesari: Ideal for a more grainy, traditional style of this rich dessert.
  • Idli & Dosa (for crispness): A small amount can be added to Idli or Dosa batters to create a crispier texture, especially in Rava Dosa.
  • Batters & Coatings: Add to pakora batters or use as a coating for fish, chicken, or cutlets for an extra-crispy finish.
  • Dhokla: Used in some recipes for steamed savory cakes like Rava Dhokla.
  • Savory Cakes (Handvo): Can be a component in savory baked dishes.

Tips for Cooking with Coarse Semolina

  • Roasting coarse semolina (moti suji) in a dry pan or with a little ghee until it is fragrant and lightly colored is a crucial first step for most recipes like Upma and Halwa. This prevents lumps and brings out its nutty flavor.
  • It may require slightly more liquid and a longer cooking time than fine semolina.

Your Stockholm Source for All Varieties of Indian Grains

Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer a full range of flours and grains for authentic cooking, including this high-quality Heera Semolina Coarse. Stock your Stockholm pantry for delicious and perfectly textured Indian dishes.

Name: Vandevi Hing Yellow Powder (Asafoetida) 50g
Short description:
Vandevi Yellow Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g - Pungent & Aromatic Spice

\n

Add an authentic, savory, and pungent depth to your Indian dishes with Vandevi Yellow Hing (Asafoetida) Powder. This 50g container holds a strong, aromatic compounded asafoetida, an essential spice for tempering (tadka) in dals, enhancing vegetable curries, and aiding digestion. A must-have in every Indian spice box.
Description:
The Unmistakable Aroma of Indian Cooking

Unlock a fundamental flavor of authentic Indian cuisine with Vandevi Yellow Hing Powder (Asafoetida) 50g. This iconic spice from the trusted Vandevi brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a cornerstone of vegetarian cooking, prized for its strong, pungent, and savory aroma that mimics the depth of onions and garlic. Just a tiny pinch, bloomed in hot oil or ghee, is enough to transform a simple dish into a flavorful masterpiece.

Understanding Hing (Asafoetida)

Hing is a dried latex (gum oleoresin) exuded from the rhizome or tap root of several species of Ferula, a perennial herb. In its raw form, it has a very strong, pungent smell, but once cooked, it mellows into a beautiful savory flavor. Vandevi's Yellow Hing is a compounded asafoetida, meaning the potent resin is blended with edible flour (like rice or wheat flour) and turmeric (which gives it the yellow color) to make it easier to use in powdered form.

  • The Ultimate Flavor Enhancer: Adds a deep, savory, umami-rich flavor that is especially crucial in vegetarian and lentil-based dishes where onions and garlic may not be used.
  • Essential for Tempering (Tadka/Chaunk): A key ingredient in the initial tempering process. Adding a pinch of hing to hot oil or ghee along with other spices like cumin or mustard seeds creates an incredibly aromatic and flavorful base for dals and curries.
  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating, and gas, and aid in the digestion of legumes and lentils, which is why it's almost always added to dal preparations.
  • Potent & Economical: A little goes a very long way. A 50g container will last for a considerable time in a home kitchen.
  • Trusted Brand: Vandevi is a well-recognized and trusted brand for high-quality, potent Hing.

How to Use Vandevi Yellow Hing

Always cook Hing to release its true flavor. Never sprinkle it raw on finished dishes.

  1. Heat your ghee or oil in a pan for tempering (tadka).
  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just the amount you can pick up on the tip of a knife) of Vandevi Hing Powder.
  3. It will sizzle and become aromatic in just a few seconds. Immediately add your other whole spices (like cumin seeds) and proceed with your recipe by adding lentils, vegetables, etc.

It's a classic addition to almost all lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), beans (like chana masala), and in the masala for savory snacks and pickles.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Spices

At Ideal Indiska, we understand that authentic flavors come from authentic ingredients. Vandevi Yellow Hing is one of those secret ingredients that makes all the difference in Indian cooking. We are proud to offer this essential spice to our community in Stockholm.

Description:
The Potent Aroma of Authentic Indian Tadka

Discover the powerful and indispensable flavor of L.G. Compounded Asafoetida (Hing) Powder 50g. This iconic spice from the legendary L.G. brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is a cornerstone of traditional Indian vegetarian cooking. Renowned for its exceptional strength and purity, L.G. Hing is prized by discerning home cooks and chefs for its ability to impart a deep, savory, umami-rich flavor reminiscent of onions and garlic, transforming everyday dishes into truly authentic culinary experiences.

Understanding Hing (Asafoetida)

Hing is a dried plant resin with a very strong, pungent aroma in its raw form. When added to hot oil or ghee, this pungency mellows into a beautiful, savory flavor that is crucial to many Indian dishes. L.G.'s Hing is a compounded asafoetida, where the pure resin is expertly blended with edible flour (like wheat or rice flour) to create a convenient, easy-to-use powder.

  • The Ultimate Flavor Enhancer: Adds a deep, savory foundation, especially vital in lentil dishes and vegetarian recipes where alliums (onion/garlic) are sometimes omitted.
  • Essential for Tempering (Tadka/Chaunk): A key ingredient in the "tadka," the process of blooming spices in hot fat. A tiny pinch of L.G. Hing added to hot oil or ghee is often the first step in creating an aromatic base for dals, sambar, and vegetable curries.
  • Aids Digestion (Traditional Belief): In Ayurveda, Hing is highly valued for its carminative properties. It is traditionally used to help reduce bloating, and gas, and is considered essential for making legumes and lentils more digestible.
  • Exceptionally Potent & Economical: L.G. Hing is known for its strength, meaning you only need a minuscule amount. This 50g container will last a very long time in a home kitchen.
  • The Trusted Brand for Hing: L.G. has built a legacy over decades, becoming the go-to brand for many who seek the most potent and authentic Hing.

How to Use L.G. Hing

Always cook Hing to unlock its true flavor. It should not be sprinkled raw on finished dishes.

  1. Heat your ghee or oil in a pan for tempering (tadka).
  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just 1/8th of a teaspoon or less) of L.G. Hing Powder.
  3. It will sizzle and become fragrant almost instantly. Immediately follow with your other whole spices (like cumin or mustard seeds) and proceed with your recipe.

It is a classic and non-negotiable addition to almost all Indian lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), bean preparations like chana masala, and in the spice mix for savory snacks like samosa fillings and pickles.

Your Stockholm Source for Legendary Indian Spices

At Ideal Indiska, we understand that authentic flavors come from authentic, high-quality ingredients. L.G. Hing is one of those powerful secret ingredients that professional chefs and experienced home cooks swear by. We are proud to offer this essential spice to our community in Stockholm.

  2. Once the oil is hot, add a tiny pinch (often just the amount you can pick up on the tip of a knife) of Vandevi Hing Powder.

  3. It will sizzle and become aromatic in just a few seconds. Immediately add your other whole spices (like cumin seeds) and proceed with your recipe by adding lentils, vegetables, etc.

It's a classic addition to almost all lentil dishes (dals), many vegetable curries (sabzis), beans (like chana masala), and in the masala for savory snacks and pickles.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Spices

Name: Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) 375g - For Upma, Halwa & More
Short description:
Heera Semolina Fine

\n

A versatile kitchen staple, Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) is perfect for a variety of Indian dishes. This 375g pack contains finely granulated durum wheat semolina, ideal for making fluffy Upma, rich Sooji Halwa, soft Idlis (when mixed), crispy Dosas, and even as a coating for fried items.
Description:
The Versatile Grain for Indian Delicacies: Heera Semolina Fine (Suji)

Unlock a world of delicious possibilities in your Indian kitchen with Heera Semolina Fine (Suji/Sooji/Rava) 375g. This high-quality, finely granulated semolina, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made from purified durum wheat middlings. It's an incredibly versatile ingredient, cherished for its ability to create a range of textures – from soft and fluffy in Upma and Idlis, to rich and decadent in Halwa, and crispy in Dosas or fried coatings.

Understanding Fine Semolina (Suji/Rava)
Semolina, known as Suji, Sooji, or Rava in India, is a coarse, purified wheat middling of durum wheat mainly used in making pasta, couscous, and sweet puddings. The "fine" variety means the granules are smaller, which affects its cooking properties and texture in dishes.

  • Fine Granulation: Heera's Fine Semolina has a delicate texture that cooks relatively quickly and results in smoother preparations compared to coarse semolina.

  • Versatile for Sweet & Savory: A true all-rounder, equally at home in savory breakfast dishes like Upma or Rava Dosa, and in classic Indian sweets like Sooji Halwa or Rava Ladoo.

  • Absorbs Flavors Well: Its neutral taste allows it to readily absorb the flavors of spices, sweeteners, and other ingredients it's cooked with.

  • Adds Texture: Contributes a pleasing, slightly granular yet soft texture to many dishes.

  • Quick Cooking: Fine semolina generally cooks faster than coarser varieties or whole grains.

  • Trusted Heera Quality: Heera is a well-regarded brand, ensuring consistent quality for your culinary needs.

 Culinary Uses for Heera Semolina Fine.

  • Upma: A classic savory South Indian breakfast porridge made with roasted semolina, vegetables, and spices.

  • Sooji Halwa / Rava Kesari: A rich and aromatic sweet pudding made with roasted semolina, ghee, sugar, milk/water, and often flavored with cardamom and nuts.

  • Idli & Dosa (often mixed): While Idli Rava (rice rava) is specific for idlis, fine semolina (Suji) is often used to make quick Rava Idlis or Rava Dosas, typically by mixing with yogurt or buttermilk.

  • Rava Ladoo: Sweet balls made with roasted semolina, sugar, ghee, and cardamom.

  • Thickening Agent: Can be used in small quantities to thicken soups or stews.

  • Batters & Coatings: Adds crispness when used as a coating for fried fish, chicken, or vegetables, or as part of a batter.

  • Porridge/Cereal: Can be cooked with milk or water to make a simple porridge for babies or adults.

  • Pasta & Gnocchi (in some cuisines): Semolina is the traditional flour for pasta.

Tips for Cooking with Semolina).

  • Roasting fine semolina (suji) in a dry pan or with a little ghee until lightly fragrant before using it in dishes like Upma or Halwa significantly enhances its nutty flavor and prevents it from becoming lumpy.

  • When adding to liquids, stir continuously to avoid lumps.

Name: Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain (Rice Pudding Mix) 160g - Traditional Creamy Dessert
Short description:
Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain

\n

Enjoy the authentic taste of creamy, traditional Kheer effortlessly with Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain! This 160g pack contains a perfectly balanced blend of rice, sugar, and often nuts & cardamom (check pack), expertly formulated by Ahmed Foods. Just add milk and simmer for a delicious and satisfying rice pudding dessert.

\n
Description:
The Timeless Comfort of Homemade Kheer, Made Easy: Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain.

Indulge in the rich, creamy, and comforting flavors of traditional Kheer (rice pudding) without the lengthy preparation, thanks to Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain 155g. This convenient dessert mix from Pakistan's renowned Ahmed Foods, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, provides a carefully blended mix of high-quality rice, sugar, and often aromatic flavorings like cardamom and nuts (please check packaging for exact inclusions in this "Plain" variant). It's designed to help you create a delicious and authentic South Asian rice pudding with minimal effort.

 What is Kheer?

Kheer (also known as Payasam in South India or Firni if made with ground rice) is a beloved milk-based pudding, a staple dessert across India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh. It's traditionally made by slow-cooking rice (or vermicelli, tapioca, etc.) with milk and sugar until it thickens and becomes creamy, often flavored with cardamom, saffron, and garnished with nuts.

  • Authentic Traditional Flavor: Ahmed Foods has formulated this mix to capture the genuine taste profile of classic plain Kheer, focusing on the creamy rice and sweet milk base.
  • Convenient & Quick to Prepare: Significantly reduces the cooking time and effort involved in making Kheer from scratch. Just add milk and follow the simple instructions.
  • Perfectly Balanced Ingredients (Typically): The mix usually contains the right proportions of rice and sugar, and if "Plain" still includes subtle aromatics, they are balanced for a classic taste.
  • Creamy & Satisfying Texture: Designed to produce a Kheer with a desirable creamy consistency and soft rice grains.
  • Trusted Ahmed Foods Quality: Ahmed Foods is a well-established brand, known for its range of authentic Pakistani and Indian food products, including dessert mixes.
  • Ideal for All Occasions: Perfect as a comforting dessert after a meal, for festive celebrations, or as a sweet treat anytime.

 How to Prepare Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain.

(Ahmed Foods packaging will provide specific instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Bring milk to a boil in a heavy-bottomed pan (typically around 1 litre of milk for a 155g mix, but check pack).
  2. Empty the contents of the Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain packet into the boiling milk.
  3. Stir well to ensure no lumps are formed.
  4. Simmer on low to medium heat for approximately 15-25 minutes (or as directed on the pack), stirring frequently to prevent sticking and to allow the Kheer to thicken to your desired consistency. The rice should be fully cooked and soft.
  5. Once thickened, remove from heat.
  6. Serve warm or chilled. Garnish with slivered almonds, pistachios, raisins, or a pinch of saffron strands if desired.

 Your Stockholm Source for Easy & Authentic Desserts.
Ideal Indiska is pleased to offer convenient dessert solutions like Ahmed Kheer Mix Plain, making it simple for you to enjoy traditional South Asian sweets in your Stockholm home.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Nestlé CERELAC

\nIntroduce your baby (from 6 months) to wholesome goodness with Nestlé CERELAC Wheat Infant Cereal with Milk. This 400g pack offers a nutritious and easy-to-digest first cereal, fortified with iron and essential vitamins. Just add water for a smooth, delicious, and balanced meal.
Description:
Wholesome Start for Your Little One: Nestlé CERELAC Wheat

Give your baby a nutritious and delicious start to solid foods with Nestlé CERELAC Wheat Infant Cereal with Milk (from 6 Months) 400g, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Specially formulated for babies from 6 months onwards, CERELAC Wheat is a gentle and easy-to-digest first cereal that provides essential nutrients to support your baby's healthy growth and development during this important weaning stage.

Why Choose Nestlé CERELAC Wheat for Your Baby?

  • Nutritionally Adapted for Babies: CERELAC is carefully designed to meet the nutritional needs of growing infants. It's made with carefully selected wheat and milk.

  • Fortified with Iron (IRON FER): Iron is crucial for cognitive development. CERELAC Wheat is enriched with iron to help meet your baby’s daily requirements. (As seen in your image "IRON FER").

  • Essential Vitamins & Minerals: Fortified with key vitamins (like Vitamin C, which aids iron absorption) and minerals that contribute to your baby's overall health and immune system.

  • Gentle & Easy to Digest: Made with a simple wheat base, making it a suitable introductory cereal for little tummies.

  • Convenient & Easy to Prepare: Simply mix with lukewarm, previously boiled water to create a smooth and palatable meal for your baby in minutes.

  • Trusted by Mothers: Nestlé CERELAC is a globally recognized and trusted brand for infant nutrition, relied upon by mothers for generations.

  • No Added Preservatives: (Typically, CERELAC does not contain added preservatives, but always verify with product packaging if this claim is critical).

Simple Preparation for a Happy Baby

Preparing Nestlé CERELAC Wheat is quick and easy:

  1. Wash your hands before preparing baby's food. Make sure all utensils are thoroughly clean.

  2. Boil clean drinking water for 5 minutes. Allow to cool. Measure 75ml (or as per pack instructions) of lukewarm water and pour into baby's bowl.

  3. Add 25g or 4-5 level tablespoons (or as per pack instructions) of CERELAC.

  4. Stir until the cereal is smooth and feed immediately using a clean spoon.
    Do not keep unfinished portions.

Vatika Coconut Hair Oil offers comprehensive care rooted in nature:

  • Pure Coconut Oil Power: Coconut oil is renowned for its ability to penetrate the hair shaft more effectively than many other oils. It helps to reduce protein loss, moisturize from within, protect against damage, and add natural softness and shine.

  • Enriched with Herbal Extracts: Vatika fortifies this coconut oil with select herbs (the specific herbs can vary, e.g., Amla, Brahmi, Henna, Lemon, Rosemary – check your product's packaging for its unique blend) that work synergistically to:

    • Nourish the Scalp: Helping to maintain a healthy scalp environment, which is crucial for healthy hair growth.

    • Strengthen Hair Roots: Aiding in reducing hair fall due to breakage.

    • Condition Hair Deeply: Leaving hair feeling soft, smooth, and more manageable.

    • Promote Natural Lustre: Enhancing the natural shine and vibrancy of your hair.

  • Complete Hair Nourishment: Provides holistic care, addressing issues from root strength to tip conditioning.

  • Trusted Vatika Formulation: Backed by Dabur Vatika's legacy of blending traditional Ayurvedic wisdom with modern science to create effective herbal hair care solutions.

  • Generous 300ml Bottle: This larger size ensures a good supply for your regular hair oiling routine.

 How to Use Vatika Coconut Hair Oil for Best Results.

  1. Pour an adequate amount of Vatika Coconut Hair Oil into your palm. (The oil may naturally solidify in cooler temperatures; if so, gently place the bottle in warm water to liquefy).

  2. Apply the oil thoroughly to your scalp and massage gently with your fingertips in a circular motion for 5-10 minutes. This helps improve blood circulation and oil absorption.

  3. Work the remaining oil through the entire length of your hair, from the roots to the tips.

  4. For optimal deep conditioning, leave the oil on for at least one hour, or preferably overnight. You can cover your hair with a shower cap or a warm towel.

  5. Wash your hair thoroughly with a good quality, gentle shampoo.
    Use 2-3 times a week for visibly healthier, stronger, and shinier hair.

Description:
Classic Gentle Care for Your Baby: Johnson's Baby Powder

Provide your little one with the timeless comfort and gentle care of Johnson's Baby Powder 200g, a globally recognized baby care essential available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. For over a century, Johnson's has been dedicated to creating products specially designed for a baby's delicate skin, and this classic powder is a testament to that heritage.

 The Johnson's Baby Powder Difference

Johnson's Baby Powder is formulated to offer gentle comfort and protection:

  • Helps Absorb Excess Moisture: Effectively absorbs moisture, which can help prevent chafing and discomfort, especially in skin folds and diaper areas, leaving skin feeling dry.

  • Keeps Skin Soft & Smooth: The fine, soft texture of the powder helps to keep your baby's delicate skin feeling smooth and comfortable.

  • Classic Mild & Gentle Formula: Developed to be gentle on baby's skin. (It's good to check packaging for claims like "hypoallergenic" or "dermatologist-tested" if present on your specific stock).

  • Iconic Baby-Fresh Scent: Features the unmistakable, clean, and classic Johnson's baby fragrance that parents have loved for generations.

  • Trusted by Millions: Johnson's is a leading name in baby care worldwide, trusted by parents and healthcare professionals.

  • Multi-Purpose Use: While ideal for babies after a bath or during nappy changes, some adults also enjoy using it for its moisture-absorbing properties.

 How to Use Johnson's Baby Powder Safely & Effectively

  1. Ensure baby's skin is clean and dry before application.

  2. Shake powder directly into your hand, away from the baby's face, to avoid inhalation.

  3. Gently apply the powder onto the desired areas of baby's skin, such as a_round_ nappy areas, underarms, and skin folds where moisture can accumulate.

  4. Close the container tightly after use.

 Important Safety Information

  • Keep powder away from baby's nose and mouth to avoid inhalation, which can cause breathing problems.

  • Avoid contact with eyes.

  • For external use only.

  • Keep out of reach of children.

  • Do not use on broken skin.

Name: Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder (Pure Lawsonia Inermis/Mehndi) 250g - For Tatto and Skin color
Short description:
Natural Henna

\n

Discover the traditional beauty secret with Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder. This 100g pack contains 100% pure, finely sifted Lawsonia Inermis (Henna) leaf powder. Perfect for creating intricate Mehndi designs for body art, or as a natural conditioner and reddish-brown colorant for hair.
Description:
Embrace Nature's Beauty: Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder.

Experience the timeless tradition and natural benefits of Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder (Pure Lawsonia Inermis/Mehndi) 100g. This premium quality powder, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is made from 100% pure, finely sifted leaves of the Henna plant (Lawsonia inermis). Revered for centuries across South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa, natural henna is celebrated for its ability to create beautiful, temporary body art (Mehndi).

 The Versatile Power of Pure Henna.

Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder offers a multitude of traditional uses:

  • Intricate Mehndi Body Art: When mixed into a paste, pure henna yields a rich reddish-brown stain on the skin, perfect for creating traditional bridal Mehndi, festive designs, or contemporary temporary tattoos.
  • Natural Hair Conditioner: Henna is an excellent natural conditioner that can help improve hair texture, add shine, reduce dandruff, and promote a healthy scalp. It coats the hair shaft, adding volume and protection.
  • Natural Hair Colourant (Reddish Tones): Pure henna imparts a natural reddish-brown to auburn tint to the hair. The final color depends on your original hair color, application time, and any other ingredients mixed with it (like indigo for darker shades, or amla for deeper reds). It's a great natural alternative to chemical hair dyes for those seeking warm, earthy tones.
  • 100% Pure & Natural: . Eco By Life aims to provide pure henna leaf powder, free from synthetic dyes, PPD, ammonia, or other harsh chemicals often found in commercial "black henna" or chemical hair dyes.

  • Promotes Scalp Health: Its natural properties can help soothe the scalp and maintain its health.

How to Use Eco By Life Natural Henna Powder.

Always perform a patch test 24-48 hours before full application on skin or hair to check for any sensitivities, even with natural products.

  • For Mehndi (Body Art):

  1. Mix henna powder with a slightly acidic liquid like lemon juice or strong black tea to form a smooth, thick paste (like toothpaste consistency).
  2. Let the paste sit for several hours (6-12 hours, or overnight) at room temperature for dye release (the top layer will turn brownish-red).
  3. You can add essential oils like eucalyptus or tea tree oil for a darker stain and pleasant aroma.
  4. Fill into a cone and apply your design to clean, dry skin.
  5. Let the paste dry completely (several hours). For a darker stain, you can apply a lemon-sugar sealant once semi-dry.
  6. Gently scrape off the dried paste. Avoid water for the first 12-24 hours. The stain will darken over 24-48 hours.

  • For Hair Conditioning/Colouring:

  1. Mix henna powder with warm water, tea decoction, coffee decoction, or yogurt to form a thick, smooth paste.
  2. (Optional) Add other beneficial ingredients like amla powder, shikakai powder, egg, or a few drops of essential oil.
  3. Apply the paste evenly to clean, dry or damp hair, from roots to tips, wearing gloves.
  4. Cover your hair with a shower cap and leave it on for 1-4 hours, depending on the desired conditioning effect and color intensity.
  5. Rinse thoroughly with water. You may or may not need to shampoo immediately after, depending on your preference (some prefer to shampoo the next day). Condition if needed.
