Description:
Wholesome & Versatile: Malik Foods Samo Seeds (Moraiyo/Bhagar)

Discover the goodness of Malik Foods Samo Seeds (Moraiyo/Bhagar/Barnyard Millet) 400g, a staple in Indian households, especially during fasting periods (Vrat/Upvas), now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. These tiny, power-packed grains are not only essential for religious observances but are also a fantastic addition to a healthy, gluten-free diet.

What are Samo Seeds (Barnyard Millet)?
Samo seeds, also known by various regional names like Moraiyo, Bhagar, Varai, Kuthiraivali, or Sanwa, are the seeds of a type of grass (Echinochloa frumentacea). Despite being called "seeds," they are botanically a type of millet. They are highly valued for their nutritional profile and quick cooking time.

Why Choose Malik Foods Samo Seeds?

  • Ideal for Fasting (Vrat/Upvas): Samo seeds are a permitted grain during Hindu fasts when cereals like rice and wheat are avoided. They provide essential energy and nutrients.

  • Naturally Gluten-Free: An excellent choice for individuals with celiac disease or gluten sensitivity, offering a versatile alternative to wheat-based grains.

  • Rich in Nutrients: Barnyard millet is a good source of dietary fiber, protein, and essential minerals like iron and magnesium. It's known to be light on the stomach and easily digestible.

  • Quick Cooking & Versatile: These tiny grains cook much faster than rice. They can be used to prepare a variety of sweet and savory dishes.

  • Quality Assured: Malik Foods is committed to providing quality grains, ensuring you receive clean and wholesome Samo seeds.

Culinary Uses for Samo Seeds:

Malik Foods Samo Seeds can be transformed into a range of delightful dishes:

  • Samo Khichdi: A savory porridge cooked with potatoes, peanuts, green chilies, and spices – a popular fasting food.

  • Samo Kheer: A delicious, creamy pudding made with milk, sugar, and flavored with cardamom and nuts.

  • Samo Upma/Pulao: A savory dish similar to semolina upma, cooked with vegetables and mild spices.

  • Samo Flour (Bhagar Flour): The seeds can be ground into flour to make dosas, idlis, puris, or flatbreads (bhakri) suitable for fasting.

  • Porridge: A simple, nutritious porridge for a light meal.

Your Trusted Source for Fasting Essentials in Stockholm:
Ideal Indiska understands the importance of traditional ingredients. We are pleased to offer Malik Foods Samo Seeds, making it easier for you to observe fasts and enjoy healthy, traditional meals in Stockholm.

\n

Achieve a stunning, natural red colour and mild heat in your dishes with TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder. This 100g pack offers finely ground Kashmiri chillies, prized for imparting a beautiful hue without overwhelming spiciness. Perfect for tandoori preparations, rogan josh, curries, and more.
Description:
The Secret to Vibrant Colour & Mild Spice: TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder

Transform your culinary creations with the beautiful rich red hue and gentle warmth of TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder 400g, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. This special variety of chilli powder, made from select Kashmiri chillies, is a prized ingredient in Indian cuisine, renowned more for the stunning natural colour it imparts than for intense heat.

What Makes Kashmiri Chilli Powder Unique?

Kashmiri chillies are longer, fleshier, and have a lower Scoville Heat Unit (SHU) rating compared to many other Indian chilli varieties. When dried and ground, they produce a powder that is exceptionally vibrant red and relatively mild in spiciness, making it perfect for dishes where color is as important as flavor.

Why Choose TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder?

  • Vibrant Natural Red Colour: Imparts a deep, appealing red color to your food without the need for artificial food colorings.
  • Mild & Pleasant Heat: Adds a subtle warmth and a distinct, slightly fruity chilli flavor that doesn't overpower other spices or ingredients. Ideal for those who prefer less spicy food but still want authentic flavor.
  • Authentic TRS Quality: TRS ensures this powder is finely ground from genuine Kashmiri chillies, delivering consistent color and mildness.
  • Versatile Culinary Use: A staple in many North Indian, particularly Kashmiri and Punjabi, dishes.
  • Enhances Visual Appeal: Makes your curries, tandoori items, and other preparations look incredibly appetizing.

Culinary Uses for TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder:

  • Tandoori Dishes: Essential for achieving the classic red color in Tandoori Chicken, Paneer Tikka, and other tandoori marinades.
  • Rogan Josh: A key ingredient in this iconic Kashmiri lamb curry, contributing to its signature red gravy.
  • Curries & Gravies: Use in various meat, vegetable, and lentil curries where a rich red color and mild heat are desired (e.g., Butter Chicken, Korma).
  • Vegetable Preparations (Sabzis): Adds color and mild spice to everyday vegetable dishes.
  • Marinades & Spice Blends: Incorporate into marinades or create your own spice mixes where a touch of red and mild heat is needed.
  • Soups & Stews: Can add a lovely hue and subtle warmth.

Tips for Use:

  • Use generously for color; the heat level is mild.
  • Can be combined with hotter chilli powders if you desire both intense color and higher spiciness.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Indian Spices:
Ideal Indiska is proud to offer TRS Kashmiri Chilli Powder, allowing you to bring authentic color and flavor to your Indian cooking adventures in Stockholm.

 Simple Heating Instructions.

(Always refer to Haldiram's packaging for precise instructions.)

  1. Do Not Thaw (Usually): Most frozen parathas are best cooked directly from frozen.

  2. Heat Tawa/Pan: Preheat a flat pan or tawa over medium heat.

  3. Cook Paratha: Place the frozen Aloo Paratha on the heated tawa. Cook for about 2-3 minutes on one side, or until golden brown spots appear.

  4. Flip & Cook: Flip the paratha. You can add a teaspoon of ghee or oil around the edges and on top if desired, for extra crispness and flavor. Cook the other side for another 2-3 minutes, pressing gently and evenly with a flat spatula, until it's golden brown, crispy on the outside, and the filling is heated through.

  5. Serve Hot: Best served immediately.

Aloo Parathas are traditionally enjoyed with a dollop of white butter (makhan), plain yogurt (dahi), a side of Indian pickles (achaar), or a simple onion-tomato salad.

Name: MTR Rasam Powder 200g - Spice Blend for Authentic South Indian Soup
Short description:
MTR Rasam Powder 200g

\nCreate authentic, tangy, and aromatic South Indian Rasam at home with MTR Rasam Powder! This 200g pack contains a perfectly balanced blend of traditional spices, expertly formulated by MTR for that signature tangy, peppery, and comforting taste. The perfect base for a delicious and soothing soup.
Description:
The Soulful & Spicy Soup of the South: MTR Rasam Powder - 200g

Experience the authentic and invigorating flavors of a South Indian culinary classic with MTR Rasam Powder 200g. This signature spice blend from the legendary MTR brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is expertly crafted with a unique combination of ground lentils and spices, including coriander, black pepper, and cumin. It's designed to help you effortlessly recreate the traditional tangy, spicy, and deeply aromatic taste of Rasam – a beloved soupy dish.

 What is Rasam?

Rasam is a light, fluid, soup-like dish from South India, known for its distinctive sour and spicy flavor profile. It's typically made with a tamarind or tomato base, along with dal water, and seasoned with a special blend of spices – the Rasam Powder. It can be consumed as a soup, an appetizer, or mixed with steamed rice.

  • Authentic South Indian Flavor: MTR's recipe, perfected over decades, captures the genuine taste of traditional Rasam, balancing tanginess, pepperiness, and savory notes.
  • Perfectly Balanced Spice Blend: Takes the guesswork out of sourcing, roasting, and grinding numerous individual spices like coriander, cumin, black pepper, red chilli, and fenugreek. MTR ensures a consistent and delicious flavor.
  • Aromatic & Comforting: The blend of spices creates a wonderfully fragrant and warming soup that is both comforting and believed to have digestive and cold-relieving properties in traditional home remedies.
  • Convenient & Time-Saving: Simplifies the cooking process by providing the essential spice foundation in one perfectly proportioned pack.
  • Trusted Heritage Brand: MTR Foods has been a staple in South Indian kitchens for a century, guaranteeing quality and authentic taste.

 How to Use MTR Rasam Powder

(MTR packaging usually provides detailed recipe instructions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Cook a small amount of toor dal until very soft. Mash it and keep aside with its cooking water.
  2. Prepare a tamarind extract by soaking tamarind in hot water and squeezing out the juice.
  3. In a pot, combine the tamarind extract, chopped tomatoes, and the required amount of MTR Rasam Powder (as per package instructions or to taste). Add salt and a pinch of turmeric.
  4. Bring to a boil and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes for the raw taste of the tamarind and spices to cook out.
  5. Add the cooked, mashed dal with its water. Adjust consistency with more water if needed. Let it simmer for a few more minutes, but do not boil vigorously after adding the dal.
  6. Prepare a tempering (tadka) of mustard seeds, dried red chillies, curry leaves, and asafoetida (hing) in ghee or oil, and pour over the Rasam.
  7. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot.

Enjoy as a soup or mix with hot steamed rice and a dollop of ghee.

  • Indian-style chai

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

The Essence of Tikka Masala

Tikka Masala is a creamy, tomato-based curry with a complex, aromatic flavor profile. While its origins are debated, its taste is universally loved.

  • Authentic Mild & Creamy Flavor: Patak's paste is carefully balanced to deliver a genuine Tikka Masala taste that is rich and flavorful without being hot, making it perfect for all palates.

  • Convenient & Time-Saving: This ready-to-use paste significantly reduces preparation time, allowing you to create a restaurant-quality meal at home with ease.
  • Trusted Patak's Quality: Patak's has a rich heritage in producing authentic Indian pastes and sauces, trusted by cooks worldwide.

How to Use Patak's Tikka Masala Paste

(Patak's jars typically provide recipe suggestions. A general guide is below.)

  1. Heat oil in a pan and brown your choice of diced chicken, paneer, or vegetables.
  2. Stir in Patak's Tikka Masala Paste (typically 2-3 tablespoons per serving) and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
  3. Add tomato puree, water, stock, or coconut milk to create the sauce. Many recipes finish with a swirl of cream or yogurt for extra richness.
  4. Bring to a simmer and cook until your main ingredients are tender and the sauce has thickened.
  5. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and serve hot with basmati rice or naan bread.

The Iconic Maggi Masala Taste

For decades, the magic of Maggi has been its simplicity and its unforgettable flavour. This pack brings you that classic experience:

  • Signature Masala Tastemaker: The heart and soul of the dish. Each pack comes with a sachet of the secret "Tastemaker" masala, a unique and aromatic blend of roasted Indian spices that creates a delicious, savory, and slightly spicy broth.

Simple Cooking Instructions

  1. Break the noodle cake into four pieces.
  2. Add the noodles and the Tastemaker sachet to approximately 210ml (1.5 teacups) of boiling water.

Description:
The Trusted Choice for Perfectly Soft Rotis, Every Day

Experience the consistent quality and wholesome goodness of Al Baker Chakki Fresh Atta (Whole Wheat Flour), a premium choice for your daily Indian flatbreads, now available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. Al Baker is a renowned brand, trusted by families for its commitment to sourcing the finest wheat grains and using a traditional chakki-grinding process to produce a superior quality atta. The result is a flour that makes kneading a pleasure and produces rotis that are exceptionally soft, fluffy, and delicious.

What is meant by "Chakki Fresh Atta"?

The term "Chakki" refers to a traditional Indian stone mill used for grinding grains. "Chakki Fresh Atta" signifies that this whole wheat flour has been ground in a way that mimics this method. This slow, low-temperature grinding process is crucial as it helps to preserve the natural dietary fiber, bran, and nutrients of the whole wheat grain. This not only makes the atta more nutritious but is also the secret to achieving that authentic, homemade taste and an incredibly soft texture in your rotis.

Why Choose Al Baker Chakki Fresh Atta?

  • Guaranteed Softness 6 hour: Al Baker's formulation and chakki-grinding process are designed specifically to produce dough that yields consistently soft and pliable rotis, chapatis, and phulkas that don't dry out quickly.
  • 100% Whole Wheat: Made from the entire wheat grain, this atta is a natural source of dietary fiber, which is essential for a healthy and balanced diet.
  • Rich in Nutrients: By preserving the bran and germ of the wheat, this atta retains more natural vitamins and minerals compared to refined flours.
  • Easy to Knead: The fine quality of the flour ensures a smooth, elastic dough that is easy to work with, whether you are kneading by hand or with a machine.
  • Trusted International Brand: Al Baker is a well-established brand known for its high standards of quality and hygiene.

Perfect for All Your Favourite Indian Breads

  • Fluffy Phulkas: Create light rotis that puff up perfectly on the flame.

Name: TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) 200g
Short description:
TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) 200g - For Bhel Puri & Light Snacks

\n

Create the ultimate Indian street food snack, Bhel Puri, at home with TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura)! This 200g pack contains light, airy, and crispy puffed rice, ready to be tossed with chutneys, onions, potatoes, and sev. Also perfect for making light savory snacks or healthy breakfast cereals.
Description:
The Light & Airy Base for Iconic Indian Street Food

Experience the quintessential crunch of authentic Indian chaat with TRS Puffed Rice (Murmura/Kurmura) in a convenient 200g pack. This simple yet incredibly versatile ingredient, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, consists of rice grains that have been puffed under high pressure to create a light, airy, and wonderfully crispy texture. Murmura is most famously known as the star ingredient in the beloved street food snack, Bhel Puri.

What is Puffed Rice (Murmura)?

Puffed rice is a type of puffed grain made from rice, commonly used in breakfast cereals and snack foods. In India, it's a pantry staple with a neutral taste that makes it a perfect vehicle for a variety of bold flavors.

  • Light, Airy & Crispy Texture: Its defining characteristic is its feather-light weight and satisfying crunch, which provides a wonderful textural contrast in snacks.
  • The Heart of Bhel Puri: Unmistakably the main component of Bhel Puri, where it's tossed with chopped onions, potatoes, tangy tamarind chutney, spicy green chutney, and topped with crispy sev.
  • Versatile Snack Ingredient: Used in many other Indian street food snacks like Jhalmuri (a spicy puffed rice snack from Bengal) and as a component in various "chivda" or "mixture" namkeens.
  • A Healthier Snacking Choice: Puffed rice is very low in calories and fat, making it a light base for snacks.
  • Ready to Use: This product is ready to be used straight from the pack.
  • Trusted TRS Quality: TRS is a well-regarded brand, known for its commitment to providing clean, high-quality staple ingredients.

How to Use TRS Puffed Rice

The possibilities are endless, but here are the most popular uses:

  • Bhel Puri: The classic preparation. Mix TRS Puffed Rice with boiled potato cubes, finely chopped onions and tomatoes, a squeeze of lemon, and a generous amount of tamarind chutney and mint chutney. Mix well and top with a mountain of crispy sev and fresh coriander. Serve immediately to retain the crunch!
  • Jhalmuri: A spicy Bengali version, often tossed with mustard oil, spices, chopped onions, chillies, and other ingredients.
  • Savory Snacks (Chivda): Can be lightly roasted or fried with turmeric, curry leaves, peanuts, and other spices to create a simple, light namkeen.
  • Light Breakfast Cereal: Can be enjoyed with milk and a little sugar or jaggery, similar to a commercial rice cereal.
  • Energy Bars & Sweets: Used in making "murmura ladoo" or "kurmura chikki," where it's bound together with melted jaggery.

A Swedish Connection?

While unique, you can think of TRS Puffed Rice as a savory, more versatile version of plain rice breakfast cereals like Rice Krispies. Imagine using that light, crispy texture not just with milk, but as the base for a vibrant, tangy, and spicy salad-like snack!

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Chaat Ingredients

At Ideal Indiska, your dedicated Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm, we are passionate about bringing you all the essential ingredients for your favorite homemade street food. With our convenient local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and all of Europe, you can easily get everything you need for your next Bhel Puri party!

Perfect for

  • masala chai
  • milk tea
  • Indian-style chai
  • morning tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

  • Indian-style chai

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Name: Laziza Chaat Masala 100g
Short description:
Chaat Masala

\n

Add an explosion of tangy, zesty, and savory flavor with Laziza Chaat Masala! This 100g pack offers a unique, aromatic blend of spices, black salt, and mango powder. It's the essential finishing spice for authentic chaats, fruit salads, dahi vadas, and for adding a distinctive kick to snacks and beverages.
Description:
The Zesty Kick of Authentic South Asian Street Food

Elevate your snacks, salads, and street food creations with the unique, mouth-watering tang of Laziza Chaat Masala 100g. This distinctive spice blend from Pakistan's beloved Laziza brand, available at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm, is an essential seasoning for anyone looking to recreate the authentic "chatpata" (tangy and savory) taste of Indian and Pakistani street food. It's expertly crafted with a complex mix of zesty, tangy, and savory spices, including the crucial ingredients of black salt (kala namak) and amchur (dry mango powder).

What is Chaat Masala?

Chaat Masala is a quintessential South Asian spice blend used primarily as a finishing garnish. Unlike other masalas that are cooked into a dish, chaat masala is usually sprinkled on top just before serving to provide a final burst of flavor. Its signature taste is a delightful explosion of savory, sour, spicy, and pungent notes all at once.

  • Authentic Zesty & Tangy Flavor: Laziza's blend delivers that signature "chatpata" taste that is irresistible in chaats, salads, and other snacks.
  • Key Ingredients for "Chaat": Contains essential components like Amchur (dry mango powder) for tartness, Black Salt (kala namak) for its unique sulphurous tang, cumin, coriander, black pepper, and other spices that define the taste of chaat.
  • Incredibly Versatile Seasoning: While a must-have for chaats, its uses extend far beyond. It's fantastic on fruit salads, vegetable salads, yogurt preparations (raita, dahi vada), sprinkled over fries or pakoras, and even in some refreshing beverages.
  • Enhances Savory Snacks: A simple sprinkle can transform everyday snacks, fruits, and vegetables into an exciting and flavorful treat.
  • Trusted Laziza Quality: Laziza is a well-regarded brand known for its authentic Pakistani spice blends and consistent quality.

How to Use Laziza Chaat Masala

Chaat Masala is best used as a finishing spice to preserve its fresh aroma and zesty flavor.

  • For Chaat Preparations: The essential seasoning for all types of chaat, including Aloo Chaat, Bhel Puri, Papdi Chaat, Sev Puri, and especially Dahi Vada/Bhalla.
  • On Fruit Salads: Sprinkle generously over mixed fruit salads (Fruit Chaat) for a delicious sweet, savory, and tangy contrast. It's particularly good on fruits like watermelon, guava, apples, and bananas.
  • On Vegetable Salads: Adds a zesty kick to a simple cucumber, tomato, and onion salad (kachumber).
  • With Yogurt: Mix into plain yogurt or various vegetable raitas to give them a tangy lift.
  • On Snacks: Sprinkle over French fries, pakoras, samosas, roasted nuts, or even popcorn.
  • In Beverages: Add a pinch to Nimbu Pani (Indian lemonade) or Jaljeera for a unique, savory twist.

Your Stockholm Source for Authentic Pakistani Spices

At Ideal Indiska, your dedicated Indian and Pakistani grocery store in Stockholm, we are proud to offer a wide range of authentic masalas from trusted brands like Laziza. With our convenient local Stockholm delivery and DHL shipping across Sweden and all of Europe, you can easily get the vibrant taste of South Asian street food delivered to your kitchen.

Name: Tata Tea Gold 1kg - Rich Taste & Invigorating Aroma
Short description:
Buy Tata Tea Gold (1kg) Online | Ideal Indiska Stockholm

\n

Shop Tata Tea Gold 1kg at Ideal Indiska, Stockholm. A unique blend of gentle long leaves & strong CTC granules for a perfect balance of rich taste and irresistible aroma in every cup.
Description:
The Gold Standard of Indian Tea

Indulge in a truly superior tea experience with Tata Tea Gold, available in a generous 1kg value pack at Ideal Indiska in Stockholm. From the house of Tata, one of India's most trusted and respected brands, Tata Tea Gold is a masterfully crafted black tea blend designed for discerning tea lovers. Its unique and innovative formula combines two different types of tea leaves to create a perfect and harmonious balance in every cup.

The Secret Behind the Perfect Balance

The magic of Tata Tea Gold lies in its distinctive two-part blend, a feature that sets it apart from ordinary teas:

  • 15% Gentle Long Leaves for Aroma: The blend contains specially selected long tea leaves which are gently rolled. When brewed, these leaves unfurl to release a beautiful, uplifting, and irresistible aroma, creating a delightful sensory experience.

  • 85% Strong CTC Granules for Taste: The majority of the blend consists of fine CTC (Crush, Tear, Curl) tea granules. This method of processing ensures that the tea releases a strong, robust, and full-bodied flavour, providing the rich taste that is essential for a satisfying cup of chai.

This combination ensures you get the best of both worlds: a tea that is as aromatic as it is flavorful.

Why Choose Tata Tea Gold?

  • A Rich and Aromatic Cup, Every Time: The unique blend guarantees a consistent balance of strong taste and delightful fragrance.

  • The Perfect Base for Masala Chai: Its inherent strength and rich flavor stand up beautifully to the addition of milk, sugar, and warming spices like cardamom, ginger, and cloves, making it an ideal choice for homemade Masala Chai.

  • Enjoy as a Strong Black Tea: The quality of the leaves also makes it a wonderful choice for those who enjoy a robust and flavorful cup of black tea.

  • Trusted Tata Brand: Tata Tea is a household name in India, synonymous with quality, freshness, and the perfect tea-drinking experience.

  • Excellent Value 1kg Pack: This large pack is perfect for families, daily tea drinkers, and anyone who appreciates a high-quality tea, offering great economy and ensuring you are always well-stocked.

A Swedish Connection: A Fika with a Twist

För våra svenska kunder som älskar en god kopp te under sin fika, erbjuder Tata Tea Gold en spännande och smakrik upplevelse. Medan traditionellt svenskt te kan vara mildare, ger detta svarta te en fylligare och mer robust karaktär, liknande ett starkt "English Breakfast"-te men med en unik indisk arom. Det är den perfekta basen för att experimentera med en chai latte, där dess styrka vackert balanserar mjölkens krämighet.

(Swedish Connection Translation: For our Swedish customers who love a good cup of tea during their fika, Tata Tea Gold offers an exciting and flavorful experience. While traditional Swedish tea can be milder, this black tea provides a fuller and more robust character, similar to a strong "English Breakfast" tea but with a unique Indian aroma. It's the perfect base for experimenting with a chai latte, where its strength beautifully balances the creaminess of the milk.)

Your Stockholm Source for India's Finest Teas
